bottom_line: Design-doc anchors must be verified against the rendered document's heading IDs before specification; the S05 review step should catch unverified anchors before QV gates flag them.
coverage_notes: Read S01, S03, S05, S06_fix1, S07_fix1, S06_run2 (tail -50 each). S09 (341KB) and S10 (308KB) sampled with tail -200 after no anomalies found in grep. S11 browser log read in full (20KB). All step self-reports read as secondary evidence. DB telemetry: full (iw-item-analyze ran with DB:UP).

findings.title: Design-doc anchor for `item_detail` was unverified, causing 2 fix cycles
findings.recommendation: Add anchor-verification instruction to CR/Feature design-doc template: for each `#anchor` in `_SLUG_TO_DOC` or any URL fragment, confirm the heading ID exists in the rendered target doc. Also add anchor verification to the S05 CodeReview_Final prompt template.
evidence: ai-dev/active/CR-00044/fix-cycles/CR-00044_S06_FIX_cycle1_prompt.md — 'The line is 103 characters. The design doc (line 45) says to verify anchors against rendered toc heading ids — if unconfirmed, ship without fragment.'
ai-dev/active/CR-00044/fix-cycles/CR-00044_S07_FIX_cycle1_prompt.md — 'The item_detail entry in _SLUG_TO_DOC had a multi-line string that ruff wanted collapsed to a single line.'
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add anchor-verification instruction to the CR/Feature design-doc template and to the S05 CodeReview_Final prompt template, so unverified `#anchor` fragments are caught before QV gates flag them. Analyzed in CR-00044 (see ai-dev/active/CR-00044/reports/CR-00044_self_assess_report.md). Effort: S.
